answer:
\boxed{B}

A **rhomboid cross-section file** (Option B) is more prone to fracture than the others because its thin, sharp edges create stress concentrations, especially when made of the same steel and size. The triangular reamer (A) and square file (C) distribute stress more evenly, while the Headstrom file (D) has a more robust design. The rhomboid’s geometry makes it inherently weaker under bending or torsional forces.
